subject: Chemistry
title: Bohr's Orbits: Electrons in Shells
description: One-minute skill: Bohr's Orbits: Electrons in Shells.

hook.text: In 1913, Niels Bohr proposed that electrons orbit the nucleus in fixed energy levels—like planets around a sun.

buildup.text: Classical physics predicted orbiting electrons should radiate energy and spiral into the nucleus. Atoms should collapse.

discovery.text: Bohr said electrons can only exist at certain distances. They jump between levels by absorbing or emitting exact energy.

twist.text: This explained hydrogen's spectral lines perfectly—each color corresponds to an electron jumping between specific levels.

climax.text: Bohr's model was wrong for complex atoms. But it introduced quantization to chemistry—a revolutionary idea.

punchline.text: Fixed orbits, quantum jumps, no spiraling in. A wrong model that changed everything.

quiz.question: What revolutionary idea did Bohr introduce to atomic theory?
options: Electrons exist only in fixed energy levels and jump between them
Electrons orbit randomly at any distance from the nucleus
Electrons are stationary and don't orbit at all
